data.title: Performant Healthcare announces preliminary Q2 results
data.description:
• Performant Healthcare (NASDAQ:PHLT) announced on Friday preliminary financial results for the second quarter of 2025.
  • The company anticipates reporting second quarter revenues of approximately $37.8M, net income of approximately $2.1M, and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $6.2M.
  • In a separate press release, Performant announced its entry into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Machinify, a healthcare intelligence company. 
  • Performant is also withdrawing its previously issued full-year 2025 guidance.

data.title: Pfizer/BioNTech lose appeal to reverse Moderna’s COVID vaccine patent win in U.K.
data.description:
London's Court of Appeal on Friday struck down a case filed by Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), in a bid to reverse a prior High Court ruling that granted a patent win to their rival Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA).

The makers of the Comirnaty COVID shot filed legal submissions last month to overturn the July 2024 ruling that determined one of Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) patents related to its Spikevax COVID-19 vaccine was valid.

At the time, Judge Richard Meade of London's High Court found that Comirnaty had infringed Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) EP’949 patent, meaning Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) were liable to pay damages to the Massachusetts-area biotech based on their vaccine sales after 2022.

However, the duo was permitted to appeal the ruling, which was upheld by Judge Richard Arnold of London's Court of Appeal on Friday.

The ruling "does not change our unwavering stance that this patent is invalid," Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) said, adding that they plan to appeal, Reuters reported, quoting a joint statement from the company. "This decision has no immediate impact on Pfizer and BioNTech or Comirnaty.”

The case marks one of many COVID vaccine-related patent disputes the two parties are engaged in across multiple countries, including the U.S.

data.title: Canada Goose breaks losing streak with Wells Fargo upgrade
data.description:
After retreating for six consecutive days off a 2-year-high, shares of Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) are set to open in the green thanks to an upgrade at Wells Fargo.

Analyst Ike Boruchow moved to the bullish camp with an Overweight rating on the outerwear, apparel, and footwear manufacturer, as “material improvements in product and marketing are driving meaningful [direct-to-consumer] growth, while wholesale upside now appears more visible.”

The turnaround has been more than 24 months in the making with production shifts, higher ad spending and a 30% cut to unproductive lines, setting up Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) for success for the first time in several years. And while yesterday’s fiscal first quarter earnings report “may have had some holes,” there was enough compelling key performance indicators within the report to suggest that investments in product and marketing have led to an inflection point.

Furthermore, the 14% slide in the stock on Thursday in the wake of its earnings report represents an attractive entry point, Burochow says, especially given double-digit DTC comparable sales.

For the fiscal first quarter ended June 29, Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) saw a 22.4% increase in total sales, with DTC sales up 23.8% and wholesale sales climbing 12%. Additionally, due to a higher number of Friends & Family events, “Other Revenue” increased more than 30%, all of which drove gross profit up 25% to C$66.2M and the company’s gross margin to 61.4%, a 170 basis points improvement from the same quarter last year.

However, due to a one-time financial settlement of C$43.8M and costs associated with expanding its global retail network, increased marketing spend for new ad campaigns, and investments in design and merchandising, the company reported a wider than expected loss of C$0.91 per share versus C$0.79 per share a year ago.

But despite the wider loss, Burochow sees the stock as “too cheap for the story that is developing,” specifically with a 6x out-year EBITDA.

“While we acknowledge GOOS still needs to invest (and likely keep margins somewhat under pressure in the near term) we still see out-year EPS power of ~C$1.50 should top-line strategies sustain.”  

After Thursday’s double-digit loss in the share price, Canada Goose (GOOS) is set to open more than 3% higher, snapping a 6-day losing streak.

data.title: Stock index futures fall as Trump's tariffs weigh; jobs report comes in lower than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the red on Friday, as President Donald Trump issued revised tariff orders, while the jobs data came in cooler than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.8%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.40%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.96%.

Nonfarm payrolls came in lower than expected in July and the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.2%

Trump on Thursday signed an executive order revising “reciprocal” tariffs on dozens of countries, setting new duty rates between 10% and 41%. According to the White House, any goods deemed to be transshipped to evade these duties will incur an additional 40% tariff.

Wall Street on Thursday limped to a negative finish as a post-earnings surge in tech titans Meta (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) was overshadowed by jitters over tariffs and economic data that showed stubborn inflation and weak personal spending.

"Equity markets have seen contrasting themes over the last 24 hours, as a resurgence of U.S. 'tech-ceptionalism' was offset by a broader loss of momentum,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

On the earnings side, Amazon (AMZN) was down 8% in premarket trade as its Q3 outlook disappointed. Apple (AAPL) was up 1.5% as it beat its third-quarter estimates, helped by higher-than-expected iPhone sales.

Other economic releases for the day include the July PMI manufacturing final and the July ISM manufacturing index. Both are expected to come in at 49.5.

The July consumer sentiment report will land during market hours.

data.title: Chevron posts Q2 earnings beat on record production, free cash flow more than doubles
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) +0.5% pre-market Friday after reporting slightly better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings, even as revenues fell to $44.37B, as lower crude prices were outweighed by quarterly records for worldwide and U.S. net oil-equivalent production.

"The completion of the Hess acquisition further strengthens our diversified portfolio and positions us to extend our production and free cash flow growth profile well into the next decade," CEO Mike Wirth said.

Q2 net income rose to $2.49B, or $1.45/share, from $3.5B, or $2.00/share, in Q1, and worldwide production totaled a company record 3.4M boe/day, up from 3.35M in Q1, as output from its Tengizchevroil affiliate surged 34%, U.S. Gulf production jumped 22%, and Permian Basin production climbed 14% to 1M boe/day.

Free cash flow more than doubled from the year-earlier quarter, up 113% to $4.9B, easily beating the FactSet consensus of $3.99B.

Q2 U.S. upstream earnings fell to $1.42B from $1.86B in Q1, primarily due to lower liquids realizations, higher depreciation, depletion and amortization and higher operating expenses, partly offset by higher sales volumes, higher natural gas realizations, and Q2 International upstream earnings fell to $1.31B from $1.9B in Q1, primarily due to lower affiliate earnings at TCO, which were largely due to higher depreciation, depletion and amortization and lower realizations, partly offset by higher sales volumes following Future Growth Project start-up.

Q2 U.S. downstream earnings rose to $404M from $103M in Q1, primarily due to higher margins on refined product sales and lower operating expenses, partly offset by lower earnings from the Chevron Phillips Chemical joint venture, and Q2 International downstream earnings increased to $333M from $222M, primarily due to higher margins on refined product sales.

Chevron (NYSE:CVX) said Q2 capital expenditure fell 7.5% Y/Y, as the company spent less on its downstream operations.

data.title: Arbor Realty Trust Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.03, revenue of $68.73M misses by $8.29M
data.description:
• Arbor Realty Trust press release (NYSE:ABR): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $68.73M (-21.9% Y/Y) misses by $8.29M.
  • Servicing portfolio of ~$33.76 billion, agency loan originations of $857.1 million.
  • Structured loan portfolio of ~$11.61 billion, originations of $716.5 million and runoff of $519.7 million.
  • Foreclosed on six loans totaling $188.2 million and sold four real estate owned properties totaling $114.5 million.
